import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

FALLBACK_INPUT = "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/orders_silver_gold/A/seed42"

CANDIDATES = {
    "order_id": ["order_id", "orderId", "orderid", "id", "order"],
    "amount":   ["amount", "amt", "price", "value", "total", "order_amount", "revenue"],
    "category": ["category", "cat", "type", "product_category", "categ"],
    "ts":       ["event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp", "timestamp", "ts",
                 "order_time", "order_ts", "created_at", "time", "order_date",
                 "event_date", "date"],
}

# Deterministic sanity window for "real" event times. Values outside this are
# treated as clearly-wrong (the "dates far in the future that can't be real").
LOWER = "2000-01-01 00:00:00"
UPPER = "2100-01-01 00:00:00"


def pick(cols, names):
    lc = {c.lower(): c for c in cols}
    for n in names:
        if n.lower() in lc:
            return lc[n.lower()]
    return None


def clean_amount(colname):
    if colname is None:
        return F.lit(None).cast("double")
    s = F.regexp_replace(F.col(colname).cast("string"), ",", "")
    s = F.regexp_replace(s, "[^0-9.\\-]", "")
    return F.when(s.rlike("^-?[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?$"), s.cast("double")).otherwise(F.lit(None).cast("double"))


def clean_string(colname):
    if colname is None:
        return F.lit(None).cast("string")
    s = F.trim(F.col(colname).cast("string"))
    return F.when((s == "") | (F.lower(s) == "null"), F.lit(None).cast("string")).otherwise(s)


def build_ts(colname):
    if colname is None:
        return F.lit(None).cast("timestamp")
    t = F.trim(F.col(colname).cast("string"))
    is_num = t.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    as_long = F.when(is_num, t.cast("long"))
    # epoch: >=13 digits -> millis, else -> seconds
    ts_epoch = (F.when(F.length(t) >= F.lit(13), (as_long / F.lit(1000)).cast("timestamp"))
                 .otherwise(as_long.cast("timestamp")))
    ts_str = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(t),
        F.to_timestamp(t, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z'"),
        F.to_timestamp(t, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(t, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(t, "yyyy-MM-dd"),
    )
    return F.when(is_num, ts_epoch).otherwise(ts_str)


def write_gold(gold):
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")
    if out_path:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet("gold_daily")


def write_silver(silver):
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    # Always try to materialize as a table the business can join against.
    try:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("silver_orders")
    except Exception as e:
        print("WARN: saveAsTable(silver_orders) failed: %s" % e)
    if out_path:
        sib = os.path.dirname(out_path.rstrip("/")) + "/silver_orders"
        try:
            silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(sib)
        except Exception as e:
            print("WARN: parquet(silver_orders) failed: %s" % e)


def main():
    spark = (SparkSession.builder
             .appName("orders_silver_gold")
             .getOrCreate())
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH") or FALLBACK_INPUT

    df = (spark.read
          .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
          .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
          .json(input_path))

    if "_corrupt_record" not in df.columns:
        df = df.withColumn("_corrupt_record", F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    orig_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c != "_corrupt_record"]
    oid_c = pick(orig_cols, CANDIDATES["order_id"])
    amt_c = pick(orig_cols, CANDIDATES["amount"])
    cat_c = pick(orig_cols, CANDIDATES["category"])
    ts_c = pick(orig_cols, CANDIDATES["ts"])

    raw_json = F.to_json(F.struct(*[F.col(c) for c in orig_cols])) if orig_cols else F.lit(None).cast("string")

    order_id_raw = clean_string(oid_c)
    surrogate = F.concat(F.lit("CORRUPT_"),
                         F.substring(F.sha2(F.coalesce(F.col("_corrupt_record"), raw_json, F.lit("")), 256), 1, 24))

    base = df.select(
        order_id_raw.alias("order_id_raw"),
        clean_amount(amt_c).alias("amount"),
        clean_string(cat_c).alias("category"),
        build_ts(ts_c).alias("ts"),
        F.col("_corrupt_record").alias("_corrupt_record"),
        raw_json.alias("_raw"),
        surrogate.alias("_surrogate"),
    )

    base = base.withColumn("is_corrupt", F.col("order_id_raw").isNull())
    base = base.withColumn("order_id", F.coalesce(F.col("order_id_raw"), F.col("_surrogate")))

    # deterministic UTC calendar day + validity of the event time
    base = base.withColumn(
        "ts_valid",
        F.col("ts").isNotNull()
        & (F.col("ts") >= F.lit(LOWER).cast("timestamp"))
        & (F.col("ts") < F.lit(UPPER).cast("timestamp")),
    )
    base = base.withColumn(
        "event_date",
        F.when(F.col("ts_valid"), F.to_date(F.col("ts"))).otherwise(F.lit(None).cast("date")),
    )

    # Deduplicate: one row per order_id, preferring the most complete/latest record.
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("amount").isNotNull().desc(),
        F.col("ts_valid").desc(),
        F.col("ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    deduped = (base.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                   .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
                   .drop("_rn"))
    deduped = deduped.persist()

    # silver: one row per order_id. Corrupt/unrecoverable lines survive here
    # (keyed by a deterministic surrogate) so they do not silently vanish.
    silver = (deduped
              .select(
                  F.col("order_id").cast("string").alias("order_id"),
                  F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("amount"),
                  F.col("category").cast("string").alias("category"),
              )
              .dropDuplicates(["order_id"]))

    # gold_daily: per-order fact used for the trustworthy per-day revenue view.
    # Excludes corrupt rows, bad/future-dated events, and unparseable amounts so
    # days are neither inflated nor polluted by impossible future dates.
    gold = (deduped
            .filter((~F.col("is_corrupt"))
                    & F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
                    & F.col("amount").isNotNull())
            .select(
                F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
                F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("revenue"),
                F.col("order_id").cast("string").alias("order_id"),
                F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("amount"),
                F.col("category").cast("string").alias("category"),
            ))

    write_silver(silver)
    write_gold(gold)

    deduped.unpersist()
    print("Run is COMPLETED")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

# Deterministic plausibility window (no wall-clock -> reprocessing is stable)
LOWER = "2000-01-01"
UPPER = "2035-01-01"


def build_ts(col):
    """Best-effort, non-throwing timestamp parse of a string column."""
    stripped = F.regexp_replace(col, r"\s", "")
    digits10 = col.rlike(r"^\s*\d{10}\s*$")
    digits13 = col.rlike(r"^\s*\d{13}\s*$")
    iso_ts = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(col),
        F.to_timestamp(col, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(col, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(col, "yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(col, "yyyy-MM-dd"),
    )
    return (
        F.when(digits13,
               F.to_timestamp(F.from_unixtime((stripped.cast("long") / F.lit(1000)).cast("long"))))
         .when(digits10,
               F.to_timestamp(F.from_unixtime(stripped.cast("long"))))
         .otherwise(iso_ts)
    )


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("orders_silver_gold")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    # Own-session configs so casts/parsers return NULL instead of throwing.
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.ansi.enabled", "false")
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.legacy.timeParserPolicy", "LEGACY")

    input_path = os.environ.get(
        "AGENT_INPUT_PATH",
        "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/orders_silver_gold/A/seed1337",
    )

    raw = (
        spark.read.text(input_path)
        .withColumnRenamed("value", "raw_line")
        .filter(F.length(F.trim(F.col("raw_line"))) > 0)
    )

    g = lambda *ps: F.coalesce(*[F.get_json_object(F.col("raw_line"), p) for p in ps])

    parsed = raw.select(
        "raw_line",
        g("$.order_id", "$.orderId", "$.id").alias("order_id"),
        g("$.event_time", "$.timestamp", "$.ts", "$.event_ts", "$.time").alias("event_time_raw"),
        g("$.amount", "$.price", "$.total", "$.value").alias("amount_raw"),
        g("$.category", "$.cat", "$.type").alias("category"),
    )

    amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount_raw"), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount = F.when(amt_clean.rlike(r"^-?\d+(\.\d+)?$"), amt_clean.cast("double"))

    ts = build_ts(F.col("event_time_raw"))
    event_date = F.to_date(ts)
    valid_date = ts.isNotNull() & (ts >= F.lit(LOWER)) & (ts < F.lit(UPPER))

    # Stable synthetic key for lines with no usable order_id (corrupt/keyless)
    corrupt_key = F.concat(F.lit("__corrupt_"),
                           F.substring(F.sha2(F.col("raw_line"), 256), 1, 16))
    order_key = F.coalesce(F.col("order_id"), corrupt_key)

    enriched = parsed.select(
        F.col("raw_line"),
        order_key.alias("order_key"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        F.col("category").cast("string").alias("category"),
        ts.alias("ts"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        valid_date.alias("valid_date"),
    )

    # Deterministic dedupe: prefer a usable amount, then a valid date,
    # then earliest timestamp, then raw line to fully break ties.
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_key").orderBy(
        F.col("amount").isNull().asc(),
        F.col("valid_date").desc(),
        F.col("ts").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("raw_line").asc(),
    )
    dedup = (
        enriched.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .filter(F.col("rn") == 1)
        .drop("rn")
    )

    # silver: one row per order, corrupt lines preserved (not vanished)
    silver = dedup.select(
        F.col("order_key").alias("order_id"),
        F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
    )

    # gold: only trustworthy, plausibly-dated, amount-bearing orders;
    # revenue = UTC-calendar-day total attached to each contributing order.
    gold_base = dedup.filter(F.col("valid_date") & F.col("amount").isNotNull())
    daily_rev = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    gold = (
        gold_base
        .withColumn("revenue", F.sum(F.col("amount")).over(daily_rev))
        .select(
            F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
            F.col("revenue").cast("double").alias("revenue"),
            F.col("order_key").alias("order_id"),
            F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("amount"),
            F.col("category"),
        )
    )

    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    if out_path:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
        parent = out_path.rstrip("/").rsplit("/", 1)[0]
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(parent + "/silver_orders")
    elif out_table:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("silver_orders")
    else:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet("gold_daily")
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet("silver_orders")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

DEFAULT_INPUT = "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/orders_silver_gold/A/seed2718"


def first_col(df, candidates):
    for c in candidates:
        if c in df.columns:
            return F.col(c)
    return F.lit(None).cast("string")


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder.appName("orders_silver_gold").getOrCreate()
    )
    # deterministic UTC calendar day
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH") or DEFAULT_INPUT
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    # ---- read messy NDJSON permissively so corrupt lines survive ----
    raw = (
        spark.read
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
        .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
        .json(input_path)
    )
    raw = raw.cache()
    raw.count()  # materialize so _corrupt_record is usable

    corrupt_c = F.col("_corrupt_record") if "_corrupt_record" in raw.columns else F.lit(None).cast("string")
    order_c = first_col(raw, ["order_id", "orderId", "orderID", "id", "order"])
    ts_c = first_col(raw, ["event_time", "event_ts", "ts", "timestamp", "time", "created_at", "order_time"])
    amt_c = first_col(raw, ["amount", "amt", "price", "total", "value", "revenue"])
    cat_c = first_col(raw, ["category", "cat", "type", "product_category"])

    base = raw.select(
        order_c.cast("string").alias("order_id_raw"),
        ts_c.cast("string").alias("ts_raw"),
        amt_c.cast("string").alias("amt_raw"),
        cat_c.cast("string").alias("category"),
        corrupt_c.cast("string").alias("corrupt_raw"),
    )

    # salvage an order_id from corrupt text so nothing vanishes
    salvaged = F.regexp_extract(
        F.coalesce(F.col("corrupt_raw"), F.lit("")),
        r'"?order_?id"?\s*[:=]\s*"?([^",}\s]+)', 1,
    )

    is_corrupt = F.col("corrupt_raw").isNotNull() | F.col("order_id_raw").isNull() | (F.trim(F.col("order_id_raw")) == "")

    order_id = F.coalesce(
        F.when(F.col("order_id_raw").isNotNull() & (F.trim(F.col("order_id_raw")) != ""), F.col("order_id_raw")),
        F.when(salvaged != "", salvaged),
        F.concat(F.lit("corrupt_"), F.sha2(F.coalesce(F.col("corrupt_raw"), F.lit("")), 256)),
    )

    # amounts recorded inconsistently -> strip formatting, safe cast
    amount = F.expr("try_cast(regexp_replace(amt_raw, '[^0-9.-]', '') as double)")

    # timestamps: numeric epochs (s / ms / us) or string dates -> safe parse
    ts_expr = """
    CASE
      WHEN try_cast(ts_raw as double) IS NOT NULL AND try_cast(ts_raw as double) >= 1e14
        THEN timestamp_micros(cast(try_cast(ts_raw as double) as long))
      WHEN try_cast(ts_raw as double) IS NOT NULL AND try_cast(ts_raw as double) >= 1e11
        THEN timestamp_millis(cast(try_cast(ts_raw as double) as long))
      WHEN try_cast(ts_raw as double) IS NOT NULL
        THEN timestamp_seconds(cast(try_cast(ts_raw as double) as long))
      ELSE try_to_timestamp(ts_raw)
    END
    """

    enriched = base.select(
        order_id.alias("order_id"),
        is_corrupt.alias("is_corrupt"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
        F.expr(ts_expr).alias("event_ts"),
    )
    # deterministic UTC calendar day; drop unreal dates by fixed bound (no wall clock)
    enriched = enriched.withColumn(
        "event_date",
        F.when(
            F.col("event_ts").isNotNull()
            & (F.to_date("event_ts") >= F.lit("1970-01-01").cast("date"))
            & (F.to_date("event_ts") <= F.lit("2099-12-31").cast("date")),
            F.to_date("event_ts"),
        ),
    )

    # dedupe: one row per order_id (prefer valid over corrupt, latest event)
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("is_corrupt").asc(),
        F.col("event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    dedup = (
        enriched.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .filter(F.col("rn") == 1)
        .drop("rn")
    )
    dedup = dedup.cache()
    dedup.count()

    # silver: keyed by order_id carrying amount, category (corrupt rows preserved)
    silver = dedup.select("order_id", "amount", "category")

    # gold: valid-day, valid-amount orders with per-UTC-day revenue
    gold_src = dedup.filter(F.col("event_date").isNotNull() & F.col("amount").isNotNull())
    wd = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    gold = gold_src.select(
        F.col("event_date"),
        F.sum("amount").over(wd).alias("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
    )

    # ---- write ----
    if out_path:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
        parent = out_path.rstrip("/").rsplit("/", 1)[0]
        silver_path = parent + "/silver_orders"
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(silver_path)
        try:
            silver.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("silver_orders")
        except Exception:
            pass
    elif out_table:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("silver_orders")
    else:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet("gold_daily.parquet")
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet("silver_orders.parquet")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
pipeline.py -- orders_silver_gold

Reads a messy NDJSON `orders` event feed and produces:
  * silver_orders  : one row per order_id carrying (amount, category)
  * gold_daily     : per-order rows enriched with UTC event_date and the
                     trustworthy per-day `revenue` (daily total).
Corrupt lines are quarantined (not silently dropped).

Deterministic: reprocessing identical input yields an identical result.
"""

import os
import sys

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql import functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

DEFAULT_INPUT = (
    "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/"
    "orders_silver_gold/A/seed3141"
)

# candidate source field names (feeds are messy / inconsistent)
ORDER_ID_NAMES = ["order_id", "orderId", "orderid", "id", "order", "oid"]
TIME_NAMES = ["event_time", "event_ts", "eventTime", "ts", "timestamp",
              "time", "created_at", "createdAt", "order_time", "order_ts", "date"]
AMOUNT_NAMES = ["amount", "amt", "value", "price", "total", "revenue", "gross"]
CATEGORY_NAMES = ["category", "cat", "type", "segment", "kind", "product_category"]


def first_col(df, candidates):
    for c in candidates:
        if c in df.columns:
            return F.col("`{}`".format(c)).cast("string")
    return F.lit(None).cast("string")


def build_spark():
    return (
        SparkSession.builder.appName("orders_silver_gold")
        .config("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
        .config("spark.sql.shuffle.partitions", "8")
        .getOrCreate()
    )


def sibling(path_or_table, suffix, is_path):
    if is_path:
        return path_or_table.rstrip("/") + "__" + suffix
    if "gold_daily" in path_or_table:
        return path_or_table.replace("gold_daily", suffix)
    return path_or_table + "_" + suffix


def write_out(df, path_or_table, is_path):
    if is_path:
        df.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(path_or_table)
    else:
        df.write.mode("overwrite").format("parquet").saveAsTable(path_or_table)


def main():
    spark = build_spark()
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH") or DEFAULT_INPUT

    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")
    if out_path:
        gold_dest, is_path = out_path, True
    elif out_table:
        gold_dest, is_path = out_table, False
    else:
        raise RuntimeError("Neither AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH nor AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE is set")

    # ---- read (PERMISSIVE, capture corrupt lines) ----------------------
    raw = (
        spark.read.option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
        .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
        .json(input_path)
    )
    # materialize so _corrupt_record is reliably populated
    raw = raw.cache()
    raw.count()

    corrupt_col = (
        F.col("_corrupt_record").cast("string")
        if "_corrupt_record" in raw.columns
        else F.lit(None).cast("string")
    )

    base = raw.select(
        first_col(raw, ORDER_ID_NAMES).alias("order_id"),
        first_col(raw, TIME_NAMES).alias("event_time_str"),
        first_col(raw, AMOUNT_NAMES).alias("amount_str"),
        first_col(raw, CATEGORY_NAMES).alias("category"),
        corrupt_col.alias("_corrupt_record"),
    )

    # ---- split corrupt vs. usable -------------------------------------
    # a line is corrupt if JSON was malformed, or it has no usable key.
    is_corrupt = F.col("_corrupt_record").isNotNull() | (
        F.col("order_id").isNull() | (F.trim(F.col("order_id")) == "")
    )

    corrupt_df = base.where(is_corrupt).select(
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("event_time_str"),
        F.col("amount_str"),
        F.col("category"),
        F.col("_corrupt_record"),
    )
    corrupt_count = corrupt_df.count()

    usable = base.where(~is_corrupt)

    # ---- normalize amount (strip currency symbols / thousands seps) ----
    amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(F.trim(F.col("amount_str")), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount = F.when(amt_clean == "", F.lit(None).cast("double")).otherwise(
        amt_clean.cast("double")
    )

    # ---- normalize timestamp (ISO strings OR epoch sec/millis) ---------
    et = F.trim(F.col("event_time_str"))
    is_digits = et.rlike(r"^-?[0-9]+$")
    epoch = et.cast("long")
    # > 1e11 seconds would be year ~5138; treat those as milliseconds
    ts_epoch = F.when(
        is_digits,
        F.when(epoch > F.lit(100000000000), (epoch / F.lit(1000)).cast("timestamp"))
        .otherwise(epoch.cast("timestamp")),
    )
    ts_iso = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(et),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_date(et).cast("timestamp"),
    )
    ts = F.coalesce(ts_epoch, ts_iso)

    enriched = usable.select(
        F.col("order_id").cast("string").alias("order_id"),
        ts.alias("ts"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        F.col("category").cast("string").alias("category"),
    )

    # a timestamp is trustworthy only inside a plausible window:
    # not before 2000-01-01, and not in the future ("clearly wrong" dates).
    valid_time = (
        F.col("ts").isNotNull()
        & (F.col("ts") >= F.to_timestamp(F.lit("2000-01-01 00:00:00")))
        & (F.col("ts") <= F.current_timestamp())
    )
    enriched = enriched.withColumn("valid_time", valid_time)
    enriched = enriched.withColumn(
        "event_date",
        F.when(F.col("valid_time"), F.to_date(F.col("ts"))).otherwise(F.lit(None).cast("date")),
    )

    # ---- deduplicate: one row per order_id (deterministic pick) --------
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("valid_time").desc(),          # prefer a usable timestamp
        F.col("ts").asc_nulls_last(),        # then earliest event time
        F.col("amount").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    deduped = (
        enriched.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
        .drop("_rn")
    ).cache()

    # ---- silver_orders : keyed by order_id, carries amount, category ---
    silver = deduped.select("order_id", "amount", "category")

    # ---- gold_daily : trustworthy per-day revenue + order detail -------
    gold_src = deduped.where(
        F.col("event_date").isNotNull() & F.col("amount").isNotNull()
    )
    day_w = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    gold = (
        gold_src.withColumn("revenue", F.sum("amount").over(day_w))
        .select(
            F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
            F.col("revenue").cast("double").alias("revenue"),
            F.col("order_id"),
            F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("amount"),
            F.col("category"),
        )
    )

    # ---- write everything ---------------------------------------------
    write_out(gold, gold_dest, is_path)
    write_out(silver, sibling(gold_dest, "silver_orders", is_path), is_path)
    if corrupt_count > 0:
        # corrupt lines must NOT just vanish -> quarantine them
        write_out(corrupt_df, sibling(gold_dest, "quarantine", is_path), is_path)

    print("quarantined corrupt lines: {}".format(corrupt_count), file=sys.stderr)
    print("Run is COMPLETED")


if __name__ == "__main__":

        .withColumn("is_future", F.coalesce(F.col("event_date") > F.current_date(), F.lit(True)))

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

RAW_PATH = "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/orders_silver_gold/B/seed1337"
CAT = "spark_catalog.default."


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(RAW_PATH)
    m = F.from_json(F.col("value"), "map<string,string>")
    df = raw.select(F.col("value").alias("raw_line"), m.alias("m"))

    def g(*keys):
        return F.coalesce(*[F.col("m").getItem(k) for k in keys])

    order_id = g("order_id", "orderId", "orderid", "id", "order", "order_key")
    amount_raw = g("amount", "amt", "total", "price", "revenue", "gross_amount", "value")
    category = g("category", "cat", "type", "segment", "product_category")
    ts_raw = g("event_time", "event_timestamp", "ts", "timestamp", "time",
               "event_date", "date", "created_at", "order_time", "order_date")

    # normalize inconsistent amount formats ($, commas, spaces, etc.)
    amt_str = F.regexp_replace(amount_raw, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount = F.when(amt_str == "", None).otherwise(amt_str.cast("double"))

    # robust timestamp parse: epoch seconds/millis OR ISO/plain string
    is_num = ts_raw.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    num = ts_raw.cast("long")
    epoch_ts = F.when(F.length(ts_raw) >= 13,
                      F.timestamp_seconds((num / F.lit(1000)).cast("long"))
                      ).otherwise(F.timestamp_seconds(num))
    iso_ts = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(ts_raw),
        F.to_timestamp(ts_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(ts_raw, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_date(ts_raw).cast("timestamp"),
    )
    ts_final = F.when(is_num, epoch_ts).otherwise(iso_ts)
    event_date = F.to_date(ts_final)

    json_ok = F.col("m").isNotNull()

    return df.select(
        F.col("raw_line"),
        order_id.alias("order_id"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        category.alias("category"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        json_ok.alias("json_ok"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="clean_orders")
def clean_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    b = spark.read.table(CAT + "bronze_orders")
    valid = b.where(F.col("json_ok") & F.col("order_id").isNotNull())
    # deterministic dedupe -> one row per order_id
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("event_date").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    return (valid
            .withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
            .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
            .select("order_id", "amount", "category", "event_date"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    clean = spark.read.table(CAT + "clean_orders").select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    b = spark.read.table(CAT + "bronze_orders")
    # corrupt / unkeyed lines are preserved (not vanished) under a deterministic key
    corrupt = (b.where(~(F.col("json_ok") & F.col("order_id").isNotNull()))
               .select(
                   F.concat(F.lit("__corrupt__:"),
                            F.sha2(F.coalesce(F.col("raw_line"), F.lit("")), 256)).alias("order_id"),
                   F.lit(None).cast("double").alias("amount"),
                   F.lit("__corrupt__").alias("category"),
               ))
    unioned = clean.unionByName(corrupt)
    # guarantee unique key
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    return (unioned
            .withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
            .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
            .select("order_id", "amount", "category"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    c = spark.read.table(CAT + "clean_orders")
    # trustworthy revenue: valid amount + plausible (non-future/non-corrupt) UTC day
    valid = c.where(
        F.col("amount").isNotNull()
        & F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
        & (F.col("event_date") >= F.lit("2000-01-01").cast("date"))
        & (F.col("event_date") <= F.lit("2035-12-31").cast("date"))
    )
    daily = Window.partitionBy("event_date")
    return valid.select(
        F.col("event_date"),
        F.sum("amount").over(daily).alias("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
    )

def _g(path):

                          _g("$.order"), _g("$.oid"))
    amount_raw = F.coalesce(_g("$.amount"), _g("$.amt"), _g("$.total"),

                          _g("$.product_category"))

                        _g("$.timestamp"), _g("$.ts"), _g("$.order_ts"),

    amount = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount_raw"), "[^0-9.\\-]", "").cast("double")

    ts = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(F.col("ts_raw")),
        F.timestamp_seconds(F.col("ts_raw").cast("long")),
    )
    return df.select(
        F.col("order_id").cast("string").alias("order_id"),

        F.col("category").cast("string").alias("category"),

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(

    # daily revenue attached to each order row
    day_w = Window.partitionBy("event_date")

        revenue.cast("double").alias("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount"),
        F.col("category"),

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T, Window

PATH = "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/orders_silver_gold/B/seed3141"
CAT = "spark_catalog.default."

# Superset schema: unknown-but-likely field names are tolerated (absent -> null,
# extra JSON fields ignored). All String so messy values never trigger read-time
# type-mismatch corruption; we clean/cast ourselves.
RAW_SCHEMA = T.StructType([
    T.StructField("order_id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_ts", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("ts", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("timestamp", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("amount", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("price", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("category", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("_corrupt_record", T.StringType()),
])


def _read_raw():
    return (SparkSession.active().read
            .schema(RAW_SCHEMA)
            .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
            .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
            .json(PATH))


def _with_parsed(df):
    order_id = F.coalesce(F.col("order_id"), F.col("id"))
    ts_c = F.coalesce(F.col("event_time"), F.col("event_ts"),
                      F.col("ts"), F.col("timestamp"))
    amount_c = F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.col("price"))

    df = (df
          .withColumn("order_id_c", order_id)
          .withColumn("ts_c", ts_c)
          .withColumn("category_c", F.col("category"))
          .withColumn("amt_clean", F.regexp_replace(amount_c, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")))

    # null-safe numeric parse (avoids CAST_INVALID_INPUT on messy amounts)
    df = df.withColumn("amount_val", F.expr("try_cast(amt_clean as double)"))

    # timestamp -> UTC calendar day
    is_numeric = F.col("ts_c").rlike(r"^[0-9]+$")
    dig_len = F.length(F.col("ts_c"))
    epoch_raw = F.expr("try_cast(ts_c as double)")
    epoch_sec = F.when(is_numeric & (dig_len >= 13),
                       epoch_raw / F.lit(1000.0)).otherwise(epoch_raw)
    date_epoch = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")),
                            F.floor(epoch_sec / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int"))
    date_iso = F.to_date(F.to_timestamp(F.col("ts_c")))
    event_date = F.when(is_numeric, date_epoch).otherwise(date_iso)
    df = df.withColumn("event_date", event_date)

    df = df.withColumn(
        "is_corrupt",
        F.col("_corrupt_record").isNotNull()
        | F.col("order_id_c").isNull()
        | F.col("amount_val").isNull())

    df = df.withColumn(
        "is_future",
        F.col("event_date").isNotNull() & (F.col("event_date") > F.current_date()))
    return df


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    return _read_raw()


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_corrupt")
def orders_corrupt():
    # quarantine so corrupt/unparseable lines do not silently vanish
    df = _with_parsed(SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "bronze_orders"))
    return (df.where(F.col("is_corrupt"))
              .select(F.col("_corrupt_record"),
                      F.col("order_id_c").alias("order_id"),
                      F.col("ts_c").alias("raw_event_time"),
                      F.col("amt_clean").alias("raw_amount"),
                      F.col("category_c").alias("category")))


@dp.materialized_view(name="clean_orders")
def clean_orders():
    df = _with_parsed(SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "bronze_orders"))
    df = df.where(~F.col("is_corrupt"))
    # one row per order: prefer plausible (non-future) then earliest event
    w = (Window.partitionBy("order_id_c")
         .orderBy(F.col("is_future").asc(),
                  F.col("event_date").asc_nulls_last(),
                  F.col("ts_c").asc_nulls_last()))
    df = df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
    return df.select(
        F.col("order_id_c").alias("order_id"),
        F.col("amount_val").alias("amount"),
        F.col("category_c").alias("category"),
        F.col("event_date"),
        F.col("is_future"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    # keyed by order_id, carrying amount, category
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "clean_orders")
    return df.select("order_id", "amount", "category")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    # trustworthy per-day revenue fact: drop clearly-wrong (future) dates
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "clean_orders")
    df = df.where(~F.col("is_future") & F.col("event_date").isNotNull())
    return df.select(
        F.col("event_date"),
        F.col("amount").alias("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount"),
        F.col("category"))

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

# Fallback dataset path for this seed (used only if AGENT_INPUT_PATH is unset)
DEFAULT_INPUT = "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/p1_medallion/A/seed42"


def build_spark():
    # We own the SparkSession. Keep it minimal to avoid unsupported-attribute issues.
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("p1_medallion")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    # UTC calendar day semantics for the contract.
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
    # Lenient time parsing: unparseable values become NULL rather than raising.
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.legacy.timeParserPolicy", "LEGACY")
    return spark


def main():
    spark = build_spark()

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # RAW LAYER
    # Read everything as strings so that a column whose values are
    # sometimes numeric and sometimes text does NOT get coerced by schema
    # inference (which would silently null-out the "text" rows and drop
    # revenue on the floor).
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    raw = (
        spark.read
        .option("primitivesAsString", "true")
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
        .json(input_path)
    )

    cols = set(raw.columns)

    # Resolve the amount column defensively.
    amount_name = next(
        (c for c in ["amount", "value", "revenue", "order_amount", "total"] if c in cols),
        None,
    )
    if amount_name is None:
        amount_name = "amount"
        raw = raw.withColumn(amount_name, F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    # Resolve the event-time column defensively.
    time_name = next(
        (c for c in ["event_time", "event_ts", "timestamp", "ts", "event_timestamp", "created_at"] if c in cols),
        None,
    )
    if time_name is None:
        time_name = "event_time"
        raw = raw.withColumn(time_name, F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    amount_str = F.col(amount_name).cast("string")
    time_str = F.col(time_name).cast("string")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # VALIDATED LAYER
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    # Robust amount parsing: strip currency symbols, thousands separators,
    # whitespace, etc., keeping only digits, sign and decimal point.
    # try_cast never raises (avoids CAST_INVALID_INPUT), yielding NULL on
    # anything that still isn't a number.
    cleaned_amount = F.regexp_replace(F.trim(amount_str), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount_val = F.expr(
        "try_cast(regexp_replace(trim(CAST(`%s` AS STRING)), '[^0-9.\\\\-]', '') as double)"
        % amount_name
    )

    # Robust timestamp parsing across multiple formats + epoch encodings.
    is_epoch_ms = time_str.rlike("^[0-9]{13}$")
    is_epoch_s = time_str.rlike("^[0-9]{10}$")

    parsed_ts = F.coalesce(
        F.when(is_epoch_ms, (time_str.cast("long") / F.lit(1000)).cast("timestamp")),
        F.when(is_epoch_s, time_str.cast("long").cast("timestamp")),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str),  # ISO 8601 / default
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS"),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS"),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "MM/dd/yyyy"),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "yyyy-MM-dd"),
        F.to_timestamp(time_str, "dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm:ss"),
    )

    validated = (
        raw
        .withColumn("_amount", amount_val)
        .withColumn("_event_ts", parsed_ts)
        .withColumn("event_date", F.to_date(F.col("_event_ts")))
        # Keep only real orders: those with a recoverable amount and a
        # recoverable event time.
        .filter(F.col("_amount").isNotNull() & F.col("event_date").isNotNull())
    )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # AGGREGATED (GOLD) LAYER
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    gold = (
        validated
        .groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum(F.col("_amount")).alias("revenue"))
        .select(
            F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
            F.col("revenue").cast("double").alias("revenue"),
        )
        .orderBy("event_date")
    )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # WRITE (idempotent overwrite -> reprocessing yields same result)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    if out_path:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").format("parquet").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        raise RuntimeError("Neither AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH nor AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE is set")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()

"""
p1_medallion — layered (raw -> validated -> aggregated) revenue pipeline.

Reads a messy NDJSON `orders` feed where amounts arrive sometimes as numbers and
sometimes as text, and event times arrive in more than one format. Lands a daily
UTC revenue total that reflects every real order.

Output contract: gold_daily(event_date DATE [UTC calendar day], revenue)
"""

import os
import sys

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql import functions as F


DEFAULT_INPUT = "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/p1_medallion/A/seed1337"


def build_spark():
    # Own the SparkSession. UTC session tz so calendar-day derivation is UTC.
    # Pure DataFrame API only (no sparkContext / _jvm access) to stay portable
    # across classic and Spark-Connect backends.
    return (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("p1_medallion")
        .config("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
        .getOrCreate()
    )


def read_raw(spark, input_path):
    """RAW layer: read every line as-is, all primitives as strings so that
    mixed number/text encodings never collide during schema inference."""
    return (
        spark.read
        .option("primitivesAsString", "true")
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
        .json(input_path)
    )


def _amount_col(raw):
    """Robustly coerce an amount that may be numeric or text (possibly with
    currency symbols, thousands separators, etc.) into a double."""
    candidates = [c for c in ["amount", "value", "revenue", "price", "total"] if c in raw.columns]
    if not candidates:
        raise ValueError("no amount-like column found in feed: %s" % raw.columns)
    col = F.col(candidates[0]).cast("string")
    # strip everything that is not a digit, dot, or minus sign
    cleaned = F.regexp_replace(col, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    return F.when(cleaned == "", None).otherwise(cleaned.cast("double"))


def _timestamp_col(raw):
    """Parse event_time from multiple formats into a UTC instant."""
    tcols = [c for c in ["event_time", "event_ts", "timestamp", "ts", "event_date", "created_at"] if c in raw.columns]
    if not tcols:
        raise ValueError("no event-time column found in feed: %s" % raw.columns)
    et = F.col(tcols[0]).cast("string")

    # epoch numeric (seconds or milliseconds) branch
    digits = F.when(et.rlike(r"^\s*\d+\s*$"), F.trim(et))
    epoch_num = digits.cast("long")
    epoch_ts = F.when(
        digits.isNotNull(),
        F.when(F.length(digits) >= 13, F.timestamp_seconds(epoch_num / F.lit(1000)))
        .otherwise(F.timestamp_seconds(epoch_num)),
    )

    return F.coalesce(
        epoch_ts,
        F.to_timestamp(et),                                   # default ISO-8601 (handles offsets)
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z'"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "MM/dd/yyyy"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "dd-MM-yyyy"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd"),
    )


def build_validated(raw):
    """VALIDATED layer: typed, normalized rows for genuinely usable orders."""
    validated = raw.select(
        _timestamp_col(raw).alias("event_ts"),
        _amount_col(raw).alias("amount"),
    )
    # keep only rows that carry a real, parseable order value at a real time
    return validated.where(F.col("event_ts").isNotNull() & F.col("amount").isNotNull())


def build_gold(validated):
    """AGGREGATED (gold) layer: daily UTC revenue matching the contract exactly."""
    return (
        validated
        .withColumn("event_date", F.to_date(F.col("event_ts")))
        .groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue"))
        .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date"), F.col("revenue"))
        .orderBy("event_date")
    )


def write_gold(gold):
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")
    if out_path:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").format("parquet").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        raise ValueError("neither AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH nor AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE is set")


def main():
    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH") or DEFAULT_INPUT

    spark = build_spark()
    try:
        raw = read_raw(spark, input_path)
        validated = build_validated(raw)
        gold = build_gold(validated)
        write_gold(gold)
        print("Run is COMPLETED")
    finally:
        spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
    sys.exit(0)

import os
import sys

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql import functions as F


# --- Configuration ---------------------------------------------------------
DEFAULT_INPUT = "s3a://ssa-spark-warehouse-<ACCT>/warehouse/_ssa_staging/p1_medallion/A/seed2718"

INPUT_PATH = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)
OUTPUT_PATH = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
OUTPUT_TABLE = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")


def build_spark():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("p1_medallion")
        # Deterministic UTC calendar-day semantics for the contract.
        .config("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
        # Legacy parser tolerates a wider variety of timestamp shapes; we still
        # guard every parse with try_* so a bad row never aborts the job.
        .config("spark.sql.legacy.timeParserPolicy", "CORRECTED")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
    return spark


def parse_amount(col):
    """Amounts arrive as numbers OR as text (possibly with $ , whitespace).

    Strip everything that is not a digit, sign or decimal point, then cast.
    A numeric value casts cleanly; a stringified value gets cleaned first.
    Nulls / unparseable -> 0 so they don't poison the SUM, but real values
    (including text-encoded ones) are preserved so revenue isn't dropped.
    """
    as_str = col.cast("string")
    cleaned = F.regexp_replace(as_str, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    cleaned = F.when(cleaned == F.lit(""), None).otherwise(cleaned)
    return F.coalesce(cleaned.cast("double"), F.lit(0.0))


def parse_event_ts(col):
    """Event times appear in more than one format. Try each candidate with
    try_to_timestamp (which yields NULL instead of raising CANNOT_PARSE_TIMESTAMP),
    then coalesce. Also support epoch seconds / millis encoded as digits.
    """
    as_str = col.cast("string")

    # Epoch handling: pure-digit strings are unix time (s or ms).
    digits_only = F.regexp_replace(as_str, r"[^0-9]", "")
    is_epoch = (as_str.rlike(r"^\s*\d+\s*$"))
    epoch_num = digits_only.cast("long")
    epoch_ts = F.when(
        is_epoch & (F.length(digits_only) >= F.lit(12)),
        F.timestamp_seconds((epoch_num / F.lit(1000)).cast("long")),
    ).when(
        is_epoch,
        F.timestamp_seconds(epoch_num),
    )

    parsed = F.coalesce(
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSXXX")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str, F.lit("yyyy-MM-dd")),
        F.try_to_timestamp(as_str),  # engine default best-effort
        epoch_ts,
    )
    return parsed


def find_col(df, candidates):
    lower = {c.lower(): c for c in df.columns}
    for cand in candidates:
        if cand.lower() in lower:
            return lower[cand.lower()]
    return None


def main():
    spark = build_spark()

    # ---- RAW: read the messy feed exactly as emitted -----------------------
    raw = spark.read.json(INPUT_PATH)

    amount_col = find_col(raw, ["amount", "value", "revenue", "order_amount", "total"])
    time_col = find_col(raw, ["event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp",
                              "timestamp", "created_at", "order_time", "ts", "time"])

    if amount_col is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Could not locate amount column in {raw.columns}")
    if time_col is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Could not locate event-time column in {raw.columns}")

    raw = raw.withColumn("_amount_raw", F.col(amount_col)) \
             .withColumn("_time_raw", F.col(time_col))

    # ---- VALIDATED: coerce messy fields into canonical typed columns -------
    validated = (
        raw
        .withColumn("amount_num", parse_amount(F.col("_amount_raw")))
        .withColumn("event_ts", parse_event_ts(F.col("_time_raw")))
        .withColumn("event_date", F.to_date(F.col("event_ts")))
    )

    # Keep only rows that represent a real order with a resolvable calendar day.
    validated = validated.filter(F.col("event_date").isNotNull())

    # ---- AGGREGATED (GOLD): daily revenue over UTC calendar days -----------
    gold = (
        validated
        .groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("amount_num").alias("revenue"))
        .select(
            F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
            F.col("revenue").cast("double").alias("revenue"),
        )
        .orderBy("event_date")
    )

    # ---- WRITE to the contract location ------------------------------------
    if OUTPUT_PATH:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(OUTPUT_PATH)
    elif OUTPUT_TABLE:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(OUTPUT_TABLE)
    else:
        raise ValueError("No output location: set AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH or AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def get_input_path():

    )


def pick_col(cols, candidates):
    lower = {c.lower(): c for c in cols}
    for cand in candidates:
        if cand in lower:
            return lower[cand]
    # substring match
    for c in cols:
        for cand in candidates:
            if cand in c.lower():
                return c
    return None


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder.appName("p1_medallion")
        .getOrCreate()
    )

    input_path = get_input_path()
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    # ---------- RAW ----------
    raw = spark.read.json(input_path)
    cols = raw.columns

    amount_col = pick_col(cols, ["amount", "value", "revenue", "price", "total"])
    time_col = pick_col(cols, ["event_time", "event_ts", "timestamp", "ts", "time", "date", "created_at"])

    if amount_col is None or time_col is None:
        raise ValueError("Could not locate amount/time columns in %s" % cols)

    raw = raw.withColumn("_raw_amount", F.col(amount_col).cast("string")) \
             .withColumn("_raw_time", F.col(time_col).cast("string"))

    # ---------- VALIDATED ----------
    # Clean amount: strip currency symbols, thousands separators, whitespace.
    cleaned_amount = F.regexp_replace(F.trim(F.col("_raw_amount")), r"[^0-9eE+\-\.]", "")
    amount = F.expr("try_cast(_clean_amount as double)")

    validated = raw.withColumn("_clean_amount", cleaned_amount) \
                   .withColumn("amount", amount)

    # Parse timestamp across multiple formats (all treated as UTC calendar day).
    t = F.col("_raw_time")
    ts = F.coalesce(
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSXXX\")"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX\")"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'\")"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z'\")"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS\")"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss\")"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, 'yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, 'MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, 'yyyy-MM-dd')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time, 'MM/dd/yyyy')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(_raw_time)"),
    )

    # Epoch fallback (seconds or milliseconds encoded as numeric string).
    epoch_str = F.regexp_extract(F.col("_raw_time"), r"^\s*(\d{10,13})\s*$", 1)
    epoch_ts = F.when(
        F.length(epoch_str) == 13,
        F.to_timestamp(F.from_unixtime((epoch_str.cast("long") / F.lit(1000)))),
    ).when(
        F.length(epoch_str) == 10,
        F.to_timestamp(F.from_unixtime(epoch_str.cast("long"))),
    ).otherwise(F.lit(None).cast("timestamp"))

    validated = validated.withColumn("_ts", F.coalesce(ts, epoch_ts))

    # Keep only real orders: parseable timestamp and a valid amount.
    validated = validated.filter(F.col("_ts").isNotNull() & F.col("amount").isNotNull())

    # UTC calendar day.
    validated = validated.withColumn(
        "event_date", F.to_date(F.from_utc_timestamp(F.col("_ts"), "UTC"))
    )

    # ---------- AGGREGATED (GOLD) ----------
    gold = (
        validated.groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue"))
        .select(
            F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
            F.col("revenue"),
        )
        .orderBy("event_date")
    )

    if out_path:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet("gold_daily")

@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():

    # we keep everything and clean downstream. Batch read -> materialized_view.
    return SparkSession.active().read.json(DATA_PATH)


# ---- VALIDATED: coerce amount + event time robustly ----------------------
@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_validated")
def orders_validated():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_raw")

    # Amount may arrive as a JSON number or as text ("12.50", "$1,234.00", ...).
    # Cast to string first (safe for either), strip anything that isn't part of
    # a decimal number, then cast to double. Empty -> null (dropped below).
    amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount").cast("string"), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount = F.when(F.length(amt_clean) > 0, amt_clean.cast("double"))

    # Event time may be in more than one format. Parse with a coalesce ladder
    # (new-parser, no session-conf mutation). Default to_timestamp handles ISO-8601
    # with zone offset (e.g. ...Z / +00:00); explicit patterns cover the rest.
    et = F.col("event_time").cast("string")
    ts = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(et),                                   # ISO-8601 (incl. offset)
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss"),
        F.to_timestamp(et, "yyyy-MM-dd"),
    )

    # UTC calendar day. to_utc_timestamp normalizes the instant to UTC before we
    # take the date, so the grain is a true UTC calendar day.
    event_date = F.to_date(F.to_utc_timestamp(ts, "UTC"))

    return (
        df.select(
            amount.alias("amount"),
            event_date.alias("event_date"),
        )
        # keep every *real* order: one with a parseable amount and a parseable day
        .where(F.col("amount").isNotNull() & F.col("event_date").isNotNull())

        df.groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue"))
        .select(
            F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
            F.col("revenue"),
        )
    )
